history.text: This public pavilion, also known as Maru Satta in Newari, was rebuilt in 1596 by Biseth in the period of King Laxmi Narsingh Malla.

The earliest Western reference to Kathmandu appears in an account of Portuguese Jesuit Father Joao Cabral who passed through the Kathmandu Valley in the spring of 1628 and was received graciously by the king.
The nine-storey tower Dharahara was originally built during this era. Rana rule Rana rule over Nepal started with the Kot massacre of 1846, which occurred near Hanuman Dhoka Durbar.

In June 2001, the Nepalese royal massacre at Narayanhiti Palace claimed the lives of King Birendra, Queen Aishwarya, Crown Prince Dipendra, and several others, and following Dipendra’s brief, comatose reign, Gyanendra was appointed king.
After the 2006 Revolution, it was turned into a museum.
The Nepali name Kathmandu comes from Kasthamandap, a building that stood in Kathmandu Durbar Square and was completely destroyed by the April 2015 Nepal earthquake (it has since been reconstructed).
On 28 February 2025, a magnitude 6.1 earthquake struck north of Kathmandu, Nepal, according to the National Earthquake Monitoring and Research Centre.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Father Cabral reported that they reached "Cadmendu", the capital of Nepal kingdom.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: During the Kirata era, a settlement called Yambu existed in the northern half of old Kathmandu.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Another smaller settlement called Yengal was present in the southern half of old Kathmandu, near Manjupattan.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: During the reign of the seventh Kirata ruler, Jitedasti, Buddhist monks entered Kathmandu valley and established a forest monastery at Sankhu. Licchavi era The Licchavis from Vaisali in modern-day Bihar migrated north and defeated.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: This created the basis of Newar Buddhism, which is the only surviving Sanskrit-based Buddhist tradition in the world.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The city served as an important transit point in the trade between India and Tibet, leading to tremendous growth in architecture.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: For example, the famous 7th-century Chinese traveller Xuanzang described Kailaskut Bhawan, the palace of the Licchavi king Amshuverma.
